name: Colorado Credit Union
description_short: Colorado Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative serving 8 Colorado counties with competitive rates on mortgages, auto loans, and savings products.
description_long:
Colorado Credit Union operates as a not-for-profit financial cooperative headquartered in Colorado, serving members across Adams, Douglas, Jefferson, Arapahoe, Weld, Denver, Broomfield, and Boulder counties. The organization has been established long enough to hold an annual meeting (noted as their 52nd Annual Meeting on the website), indicating decades of operational history in the Colorado market.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of banking products including checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), money market accounts, mortgage loans, auto loans, and home equity lines of credit (HELOC). Members can access online banking, mobile banking with mobile deposit functionality, online loan payment processing, and financial calculators. Current advertised rates include mortgages as low as 6.388% APR, auto loans starting at 4.99% APR, HELOCs at 6.75% APR, CDs at 3.70% APY, and money market accounts at 2.60% APY (as of January 2026).

As a credit union rather than a traditional bank, Colorado Credit Union operates on a member-ownership model where earnings are returned to members through competitive rates. The organization emphasizes local community involvement and provides free financial literacy resources and tools. Membership eligibility is broad, including anyone living, working, attending school, or worshiping in the service area, family members of existing members, employees of select employer groups, and members of Consumers United Association. Membership requires opening a savings account with a minimum $5 deposit.

The primary limitation is geographic—membership requires connection to the 8-county Colorado service area (or family relationship to a member). While the credit union offers competitive rates and free checking with no minimum balance, the website does not provide details on loan approval rates, funding timelines, or comprehensive fee structures. The organization appears well-established and community-focused but operates with the typical constraints of a regional credit union rather than a national lender.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Member-owned cooperative structure means earnings are returned to members through competitive rates on loans and savings
No minimum balance requirement on free checking accounts with free debit card
Free online banking, mobile banking, and mobile deposit services for all members
90-day no-payment promotional period available on new and refinanced auto loans
Broad membership eligibility including living/working/attending school in 8-county service area, plus family member access
Low introductory rates: mortgages from 6.388% APR, auto loans from 4.99% APR, HELOCs from 6.75% APR
Free financial literacy resources, calculators, and community involvement programs available to members
cons: Geographic restriction: membership limited to 8 Colorado counties unless you are a family member of an existing member
Website does not disclose approval rates, actual funding timelines, or complete fee schedule for products
Limited information about business banking or commercial lending products for small business owners
No mention of credit card products, meaning members may need to bank elsewhere for rewards credit cards
Advertised rates carry disclaimer that 'not all will qualify for the lowest rate,' suggesting many members receive higher rates
best_for: Colorado residents in the 8-county service area (Adams, Douglas, Jefferson, Arapahoe, Weld, Denver, Broomfield, Boulder) seeking competitive mortgage or auto loan rates
Members prioritizing free checking with no minimums and free mobile banking/deposit capabilities
Homeowners in the service area interested in home equity loans or lines of credit at competitive rates
Individuals who value member-ownership model and prefer community-focused financial institutions over large national banks
diagnosis: Colorado Credit Union is best for Colorado residents in its 8-county service area who prioritize competitive rates on mortgages and auto loans combined with free checking and member-owned cooperative benefits. The primary caveat is strict geographic eligibility—membership requires living, working, attending school, or worshiping in specific counties, though family members of existing members can join regardless of location.
services: Free checking accounts with no minimum balance and no service fees
Savings accounts and money market accounts (currently 2.60% APY)
Auto loans including new, used, and refinance options (advertised from 4.99% APR with 90-day deferred payment)
Home Equity Lines of Credit (advertised from 6.75% APR)
Mobile banking with mobile deposit functionality
Free financial literacy resources and education
Free financial calculators for member use
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
